title: Metadata Inspection Tool
content:
This script provides a command-line utility to inspect Python package metadata within the current environment.

### Purpose

The `diagnose.py` script is designed to help developers understand which Python packages are discoverable by `importlib-metadata` from various locations in the system's Python path.

### Key Exports/Functions

*   `inspect(path)`: Takes a file system path and discovers all Python distributions (`importlib_metadata.Distribution`) found at that path. It then prints the number of packages found and their names.
*   `run()`: Iterates through all entries in `sys.path` and calls `inspect()` for each path.

### Usage Example

To run this script and see what packages are detected in your Python environment, you can execute it directly:

```bash
python -m importlib_metadata.diagnose
```

This will print output similar to:

```
Inspecting /path/to/your/python/site-packages
Found 10 packages: package1, package2, ...
Inspecting /another/path
Found 0 packages:
```
